perturbation.original_text: The Adviser hereby agrees to indemnify and save harmless FASC and its trustees, officers and employees (each of the foregoing an "Indemnified Party" and collectively the "Indemnified Parties") against and from any and all losses, obligations, penalties, actions, judgments and suits and other costs, expenses and disbursements of any kind or nature whatsoever which may be imposed on, incurred by or asserted against an Indemnified Party, other than as a consequence of gross negligence or willful misconduct on the part of an Indemnified Party, arising out of or in connection with this Limited Power of Attorney or any other agreement, instrument or document executed in connection with the exercise of the authority granted to FASC herein to act on behalf of the Adviser, including without limitation the reasonable costs, expenses and disbursements in connection with defending such Indemnified Party against any claim or liability related to the exercise or performance of any of FASC's powers or duties under this Limited Power of Attorney or any of the other agreements, instruments or documents executed in connection with the exercise of the authority granted to FASC herein to act on behalf of the Adviser, or the taking of any action under or in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: The Adviser hereby agrees to indemnify and save harmless FASC and its trustees, officers and employees (each of the foregoing an "Indemnified Party" and collectively the "Indemnified Parties") against and from any and all losses, obligations, penalties, actions, judgments and suits and other costs, expenses and disbursements of any kind or nature whatsoever which may be imposed on, incurred by or asserted against an Indemnified Party arising out of or in connection with this Limited Power of Attorney or any other agreement, instrument or document executed in connection with the exercise of the authority granted to FASC herein to act on behalf of the Adviser, including without limitation the reasonable costs, expenses and disbursements in connection with defending such Indemnified Party against any claim or liability related to the exercise or performance of any of FASC's powers or duties under this Limited Power of Attorney or any of the other agreements, instruments or documents executed in connection with the exercise of the authority granted to FASC herein to act on behalf of the Adviser, or the taking of any action under or in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "other than as a consequence of gross negligence or willful misconduct on the part of an Indemnified Party", the indemnification clause now requires the Adviser to indemnify FASC even for FASC's own gross negligence or willful misconduct. This creates a legal contradiction because such broad indemnification clauses are often restricted or prohibited by state laws.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Pennsylvania law generally prohibits indemnification for willful misconduct or gross negligence.
perturbation.law_explanation: Pennsylvania law generally disallows contracts that indemnify a party for their own gross negligence or willful misconduct. The modification removes the exception for gross negligence or willful misconduct, forcing Federated Investment Management Company to indemnify FASC even in those scenarios. This contradicts the implied limitations under Pennsylvania law regarding the scope of indemnification agreements and introduces legal risk.
perturbation.justification: The modification removes the exception for gross negligence or willful misconduct, requiring the Adviser to indemnify FASC even in those scenarios. The Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation detail how this contradicts the implied limitations regarding the scope of indemnification agreements under Pennsylvania law. While no specific law snippets were provided, the absence of an exception for gross negligence or willful misconduct, as detailed in the explanations, leads to a potentially unenforceable indemnification clause. This omission directly contradicts typical legal standards regarding indemnification.
